exercises.title: Simulate HTTP Response Parsing
exercises.description: Simulate what you'd do with an HTTP response from the requests library. Parse JSON response data, check status codes, and extract headers from a mock response object.
exercises.starterCode:
import json

# Simulate a requests.Response object
class MockResponse:
    def __init__(self, status_code, json_data, headers=None):
        self.status_code = status_code
        self._json_data = json_data
        self.headers = headers or {}
        self.text = json.dumps(json_data)
        self.ok = 200 <= status_code < 300

    def json(self):
        return self._json_data

    def raise_for_status(self):
        if not self.ok:
            raise Exception(f"HTTP Error: {self.status_code}")


# Simulate a GET /users/1 response
response = MockResponse(
    status_code=200,
    json_data={
        "id": 1,
        "name": "Alice Smith",
        "email": "alice@example.com",
        "role": "admin",
        "active": True
    },
    headers={"Content-Type": "application/json", "X-Request-Id": "abc123"}
)

# TODO: Check response.ok and print: "Status: 200 OK"

# TODO: Parse the JSON and print each field
# Format: "id: 1"
# Format: "name: Alice Smith"
# (print all 5 fields)

# TODO: Print the Content-Type header
# Format: "Content-Type: application/json"

# TODO: Simulate a 404 response and handle the exception
error_response = MockResponse(404, {"error": "Not found"})
# Call raise_for_status() inside a try/except and print: "Error: HTTP Error: 404"
exercises.expectedOutput:
Status: 200 OK
id: 1
name: Alice Smith
email: alice@example.com
role: admin
active: True
Content-Type: application/json
Error: HTTP Error: 404
hints: for key, value in data.items(): print(f'{key}: {value}')
try: error_response.raise_for_status() except Exception as e: print(f'Error: {e}')

# Test build_url
url1 = build_url("https://api.example.com", "/users",
                  {"page": 1, "per_page": 10, "sort": "name"})
print(url1)

url2 = build_url("https://api.example.com", "/search",
                  {"q": "python tutorial", "lang": "en"})

parsed = parse_query("https://api.example.com/users?page=2&per_page=5&sort=email")
print(parsed["page"])
print(parsed["sort"])

exercises.title: Simulate a REST API Client
exercises.description: Build a mock REST API client class that simulates CRUD operations (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) against an in-memory data store instead of a real HTTP server.
exercises.starterCode:
import json

class MockAPIClient:
    """Simulates an HTTP REST client backed by in-memory storage."""

    def __init__(self, base_url):
        self.base_url = base_url
        self._store = {}  # resource_type -> {id -> record}
        self._next_id = 1

    def _make_response(self, status, data):
        return {"status": status, "data": data}

    def get(self, resource, resource_id=None):
        # TODO: If resource_id given, return that record (404 if not found)
        # TODO: Otherwise return all records for that resource type
        pass

    def post(self, resource, data):
        # TODO: Assign a new id to data, store it, return 201 + new record
        pass

    def put(self, resource, resource_id, data):
        # TODO: Update existing record, return 200 + updated record
        # Return 404 if not found
        pass

    def delete(self, resource, resource_id):
        # TODO: Delete record, return 204 + {"deleted": True}
        # Return 404 if not found
        pass


client = MockAPIClient("https://api.example.com")

# Create users
r = client.post("users", {"name": "Alice", "email": "alice@example.com"})
print(f"Created: {r['status']} - {r['data']}")

r = client.post("users", {"name": "Bob", "email": "bob@example.com"})
print(f"Created: {r['status']} - {r['data']}")

# List all
r = client.get("users")
print(f"List: {r['status']} - {len(r['data'])} users")

# Get one
r = client.get("users", 1)
print(f"Get: {r['status']} - {r['data']['name']}")

# Update
r = client.put("users", 1, {"name": "Alice Smith", "email": "alice@example.com"})
print(f"Updated: {r['status']} - {r['data']['name']}")

# Delete
r = client.delete("users", 2)
print(f"Delete: {r['status']} - {r['data']}")

# 404 case
r = client.get("users", 99)
print(f"Not found: {r['status']}")
hints: if resource not in self._store: self._store[resource] = {}
def post: data['id'] = self._next_id; self._next_id += 1; self._store[resource][data['id']] = data
def get with id: record = self._store.get(resource, {}).get(resource_id); return 200 or 404
def put: if resource_id not in self._store.get(resource, {}): return 404

warmup.title: Quick Recall: urllib.parse
warmup.description: What does this code print? Trace the URL parsing.
warmup.starterCode:
from urllib.parse import urlparse

url = "https://api.example.com:8080/v1/users?page=2&sort=name#top"
p = urlparse(url)
print(p.scheme)
print(p.netloc)
print(p.path)
print(p.query)
